option.b: National Institute of Policy and Strategic Studies

solution: An indication that Africa is still the center-peice of Nigeria's foreign policy was the creation of the National Institute of Policy and Strategic Studies

question: Which of the following served as the Secretary-General of the United Nations?
solution:
Javier Perez de Cuellar of Peru served as the Secretary-General of the United Nations Organization from 1982-1992.

Others were:

Trygie Lie of Norway (1946-53), Dag Hammarskjold of Sweden (1953-61), U. Thant of Burma (1961-72), Kurt Waldeim of Austria (1972-82), Boutros Ghali of Egypt (1993-93). The current Secretary General of the Organization is Dr. Koffi Annan of Ghana.

question: A Security Council resolution requires the concurrence of
solution: In the United Nations Security Council (UNSC), a resolution requires the approval of at least 9 out of the 15 council members including the concurring votes of all 5 permanent members (United States, United Kingdom, China, Russia, and France). This means that while 9 votes are necessary, a single veto from any of the 5 permanent members can block the resolution.

question: Under the umbrella of the OAU, Nigeria mediated the dispute between
solution: Nigeria mediated in the dispute between Ethiopia and Somalia under the umbrella of OAU.

question: The atomic bomb tests carried out around the Sahara Desert made Nigeria sever relations with
solution: The atomic bomb tests carried out around the Sahara Desert made Nigeria sever relations with France

question: The political party formed in 1923 by Herbert Macaulay was

option.b: National Council of Nigeria and the Cameroons

solution: Herbert Macaulay, regarded as the father of Nigerian nationalism, founded the Nigerian National Democratic Party (NNDP) in 1923. It was the first political party in Nigeria and aimed to advocate for Nigerian interests under British colonial rule

question: The commission established to handle the electoral activities in the Second Republic of Nigeria was
solution:
The commission established to handle the electoral activities in the Second Republic of Nigeria was FEDECO.

The establishment of FEDECO was one of the arrangements made by the military to return the government to civilian rule on 1st of October, 1979.

It was established by Murtala/Obasanjo's regime. The Chairman of FEDECO was Mr. Michael Ani.

question: The failure of the assimilation policy necessitated of the introduction of
solution: The failure of the assimilation policy necessitated the introduction of association. The policy of association and assimilation were used by France to administer her colonies.

question: The head of the Nigerian judiciary is the

option.b: President of the Court of Appeal

solution: The Chief Justice of the Federation is Head of Nigerian Judiciary.

question: Under the 1999 Constitution,the appointment of ministers and ambassadors must be ratified by the
solution: The appointment of ministers and ambassadors must be ratified by the Senate Under the 1999 Constitution, While the President is responsible for the appointment or nomination.

question: A major function of the State Independent Electoral Commission in Nigeria is the

option.b: conduct of elections into local government offices

solution: A major function of the State Independent Electoral Commission in Nigeria is the conduct of elections into local government offices. The local government offices are Chairman and Councilors.

question: In a first-past-the-post electoral system, a candidate is declared when he

option.a: records the highest number of votes cast
option.b: obtains a two-thirds majority of the votes cast
option.c: obtains one-third of the votes cast
option.d: scors the aggregates of the opponent

solution: In a first-past-the-post electoral system, a candidate is declared when he records the highest number of votes cast. This system is also know as plurality system or simple majority. In this elected candidate must secure majority of the votes cast.

question: The major function of civil service is

option.b: keeping records of the activities of government
option.c: promoting the interest of civil servants
option.d: advising government and implementing its policies

solution: The primary function of the civil service is to advise the government on policy formulation (based on technical expertise and administrative experience) and implement enacted policies efficiently.

question: Citizenship acquired through marriage is referred to as
solution:
Citizenship acquired through marriage is referred to as registration.

This is a method by which women acquire citizenship through marriage. For example, a Cameroonian woman married to a Nigerian may register as a Nigerian and should take the oath of alliance and renounce her Cameroonian citizenship.

question: On the basis of its structure,a political party can be classified as indirect if

option.a: its membership is acquired through other groups
option.c: it campaigns for votes through agents

solution: On the basis of its structure,a political party can be classified as indirect if its membership is acquired through other groups

question: The final stage in the process of enacting legislation is
solution:
The final stage in the process of enacting legislation is assent of the bill.

Third reading: In this final stage, a thorough look would be taken at the bill in order to correct certain errors connected with the drafting or amendment. A final vote is also taken on the bill at this stage. The bill now taken to the President for his assent after the bill had passed all the five stages is signed by the President.

question: The study of government essentially facilitates the understanding of the

option.a: functioning of the entire social formation
option.b: organization of the executive arm of government

solution: The study of government will shield  us from ignorance towards the body governing our country. It is really important for anyone to know, how his/her country is being managed. Of course, when we know a lot about the systems governing our country, then, we will also know our responsibilities as a citizen of that country, enlightens citizens on their basic fundamental human rights and avoid the abuse of such rights.

question: In the Judicial administration, the term " bench" refers to the
solution: In the Judicial administration, the term " bench" refers to the judges.

question: Serfs are the dominated class under
solution: Serfs are the dominated class under feudalism. The first class is the king who was regarded as the sole owner of the land. The Serfs were the actual cultivators of the land.

question: The ultimate authority in a state is
solution: Sovereignty is the ultimate authority in a state. It should be noted that sovereignty is absolute power of the state to exercise supreme legal authority over its own affairs within its territory without any form of external control.

question: The most important aspect of political participation in a democracy is
solution:
voting in elections is the most important aspect of political participation in a democracy.

Membership of a political party makes one to see it as a must to vote at elections and also fights vigorously to see that others vote for his party.

He endeavors to attend many political rallies. A person with good conscience tries as much as possible to observe most electoral rules.

The person also stands the good chance of contesting at elections.

question: Under a parliamentary system of government, the cabinet holds office at the pleasure of the
solution:
Under a parliamentary system of government,the cabinet holds office at the pleasure of the legislature.

Under the parliamentary system of government, the Prime Minister is the Head of government while the King, Queen or ceremonial President is the Head of State.

The Chairman or leader of the party that won the highest number of seats in the legislature becomes the Prime Minister. He, the Prime Minister forms the government according to the constitution.

The Prime Minister can only choose his ministers from his party men in the legislature. The executive can be removed from office by the legislature. This could happen if the legislature passes a vote of no confidence in the executive.

question: The first successful disengagement of the military from politics in Nigeria was carried out by the government of
solution: The first successful disengagement of the military from politics in Nigeria occurred under the Murtala/Obasanjo regime (1975–1979). This transition culminated in the handover of power to a democratically elected civilian government on October 1, 1979, marking the end of military rule and the beginning of Nigeria’s Second Republic

question: In local government administration, Chief and Council implies that

option.a: the council operates independently of the chief authority
option.b: members of the local council appoints the chief to the office
option.c: all members of the local council are appointed and controlled by the chief
option.d: the chief is a member of the local council but he does not exercise veto power

solution: In many traditional local government systems, especially in contexts like First Nations governance, the structure referred to as "Chief and Council" means that the chief is elected as part of the council. While the chief often acts as a symbolic or coordinating leader, he does not have unilateral control or veto power over the council's decisions. Instead, decisions are made collectively by the council, ensuring a balance of power among its members. This distinguishes the "Chief and Council" model from systems where one leader might have overriding authority over an independent council.

question: The Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries came into existence at a conference held in
solution:
The Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries came into existence at a conference held in Baghdad.

According to G.A.I. Ofoegbu, the successful nationalization of oil industries by Mexico and Iran contributed to the foundation of OPEC.

As a result of the above developments, Iraq, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, Iran and Venezuela held a conference in Baghdad, Iraq on September 14, 1960 to lay the foundation of OPEC. Again so many authors commonly agreed that the governments of Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia and Venezuela met in Baghdad on 14th September, 1960, and founded the Organization of Petroleum Exporting countries (OPEC)

question: In which of the following countries has the UN carried out peace-keeping operations?
solution:
The UN has carried out peace keeping operations in Cyprus, Somalia and Yugoslavia .

Comprehensive Government for West African Senior School Certificate by E.O. Ibezim (2002) maintained that one of the achievements of the United Nations Organization was the question of effecting peace-keeping operations in Congo, Cyprus, the Middle East and in Somalia while Essential Government by Dible C. Chris (2003) has Yugoslavia, Somalia, Lebanon, etc.

question: A key characteristics of the civil service is
solution:
A key characteristic of the civil service is Merit System.

Recruitment into the Civil Service should be based on merit and not on quota or favouritism.

This is to enable the Civil Servants to perform their functions very well and then ensure the success of the government.

question: The right that enables citizens to express their electoral preferences is
solution:
Franchise the right that enables citizens to express their electoral preferences.

It should be noted that there are two types of franchise which are as follows:

1. Restricted Franchise or Limited Franchise

2. Unlimited Franchise or Universal Adult Suffrage.

question: Equality before the law is a component of
solution: "Equality before the law" is a core component of the rule of law, which ensures that all individuals and institutions—including the government—are subject to and accountable under the law. This principle mandates that laws are applied impartially, without favoritism or discrimination based on status, wealth, or power.

question: An institution that transforms a myriad of demands into a more manageable proposal is the
solution: An institution that transforms a myriad of demands into a more manageable proposal is the Government.

question: An advantage of delegated legislation is
solution:
Efficiency is an advantage of delegated legislation.

Delegated Legislation makes for efficiency and precision. It helps to deal with issues that require some technicalities. When a subject is highly technical and requires some expert knowledge, the parliament may delegate to the Ministers, the powers to make laws or the issue, so to be able to achieve efficiency.

question: A system of government that promotes the rights and freedom of the citizens is called
solution:
A system of government that promotes the rights and freedom of the citizens is called Democracy.

Democracy is a government where people exercise power of governance through representatives. The representative must come into govern through popular, periodic free and fair elections.

According to Abraham Lincoin, ''democracy is the government of the people, by the people and for the people.''

question: In the presidential system of government , the leader is basically the head of state and
solution:
In the presidential system of government , the leader is basically the head of state and government.

The Presidential system of government is the type of political organization in which the leader popularly known as the President is the head of state in reality. In short, the President is both head of state and that of government. He then combines both the executive and ceremonial functions. He comes into power through free and fair periodic election.

question: In the process of implementing laws, the executive sometimes performs
solution:
In the process of implementing laws, the executive sometimes performs Judicial Functions.

1. The executive can grant prerogative of mercy to convicts, especially those that have overstayed or stayed reasonable part of their term in detention.

2. They executive also sets up judicial panels of inquiry outside the control of the ordinary courts of the land.

question: Which of the following justifies the Afro-centric foreign policy of Nigeria?

option.a: Her location in the West African sub-region
option.b: Her colonization by a foriegn power
option.c: Her physical size, population and resource endowments
option.d: Her ability to contribute to peace keeping missions

solution:
Her ability to contribute to peace keeping missions justifies the Afro-centric foreign policy of Nigeria.

was highly committed to peaceful settlement of political crises and inter state disputes.

She played very prominent role in the settlement of political crises in Liberia and Sierra Leone through the formation of ECOMOG.

question: Nigeria hosted the Commonwealth conference which eventually led to the independence of
solution:
Nigeria hosted the Commonwealth conference which eventually led to the independence of Zimbabwe.

When Nigeria was plunging very deep into political crises, the Nigerian Prime Minister, Sir Abubakar Tafawa Balewa preferred to focus all attention on the Rhodesian crisis. The country actually hosted a Commonwealth meeting on the Rhodesian question till the 15th of January, 1966.

question: The first military coup in Nigeria toppled the government of
solution: The first military coup in Nigeria occurred on January 15, 1966, led by a group of army officers (including Major Chukwuma Nzeogwu). This coup overthrew the First Republic civilian government headed by Prime Minister Sir Abubakar Tafewa Balewa, who was killed during the coup.

question: Adjudication of conflict between the federal and regional governments in the First Republic was reserved for
solution: Adjudication of conflict between the federal and regional governments in the First Republic was reserved for Supreme Court

question: The officer in charge of the administration of the palace in the Yoruba political structure is the
solution: In the Yoruba political structure, particularly within the Oyo Empire, the Ilari were palace officials appointed by the Alaafin (king) to manage administrative duties within the palace. They acted as emissaries, enforcers of royal decrees, and overseers of palace operations, ensuring the smooth functioning of daily activities and ceremonies.

question: Prior to 1966,the system of local government in western region was based on the three-tier structure of

option.a: native authority, division and district councils

solution: Prior to 1966, the system of local government in western region was based on the three-tier structure of Division, District and Local Councils. In Western Nigeria, there was the 1952 ordinance which established three tier system---divisional, district and local councils. Each tier was given responsibilities according to its resources, with the divisional councils shouldering the greatest responsibilities.

question: A major shortcoming of the Lyttelton Constitution was that

option.a: the Governor-General continued to head the government
option.b: no provision was made for the office of the prime minister
option.c: it failed to provide for the office of a premier
option.d: A major shortcoming of the Lyttelton Constitution was that

solution: The Lyttleton Constitution of 1954, while introducing Nigeria’s first federal structure, faced criticism for failing to establish the office of a Prime Minister at the federal level. Instead, executive authority remained vested in the Governor-General, who chaired the Federal Executive Council and retained veto powers over legislation

question: The option A-4 voting system was introduced into the political process in Nigeria during the
solution:
The option A-4 voting system was introduced into the political process in Nigeria during the Third Republic.

It was introduced by the then Chairman of the National Electoral Commission (N.E.C), Professor Humphrey Nwosu.

question: In orientation, early nationalism in Nigeria was essentially
solution: In the context of early nationalism in Nigeria, the orientation was generally reformist. Early nationalism in Nigeria was often a response to the socio-political and economic conditions imposed by colonial rule. The nationalist movements sought to address grievances and push for reforms in response to the challenges posed by colonialism rather than aiming for a revolutionary or radical overhaul.

question: Which of the following formalized colonial penetration into Africa?
solution: The Berlin Conference (1884–1885) formalized European colonial penetration into Africa. Convened by Otto von Bismarck, it established rules for the partition of Africa among European powers, leading to the Scramble for Africa

question: The 1989 Nigerian Constitution provided for a
solution:
The 1989 Nigerian Constitution provided for a Two-Party System.
This made General Ibrahim Babangida to float two national parties on behalf of government where all politicians had to fit in.

The political parties were as follows:
1. The Social Democratic Party (SDP)
2. National Republican Convention (NRC)

question: Which of the following is saddled with the conduct of the general elections in Nigeria's Fourth Republic?
solution: The Fourth Republic is the current republican government of Nigeria. Since 1999 it has governed the country according to the fourth republican constitution. INEC conducts elections

question: Public corporations are established to
solution: Public corporations are established to improve provision of social amenities Public corporations are set up mainly to provide essential services for the people. For instance, electricity supply, transport service, water supply etc.

question: A major problem associated with the Nigerian federalism is
solution: A major problem associated with the Nigerian federalism is ethnic conflicts. There has been inter ethnic contact in the various regions but without discrimination and bitter experiences. The issue of neutralization of Lagos in 1953 would have strongly made Chief Obafemi Awolowo to lead the Yorubas to secede from Nigeria but for the threat of war from the Governor and the Colonial office in London. The real secession which was experienced in Nigeria was that of the then Eastern Region in the year 1967. The secession was as a result of the 1966 military coup which was followed by the pogrom that was perpetrated against the Igbos in the North. The East declared the Republic of Biafra and engaged Nigeria in a civil war from 1967-1970. This question of ethnic conflicts has also given rise to other serious issues such has unending demand for creating more states and acceptable revenue allocation formula.

question: Privatization and commercialization programmes in Nigeria have
solution:
Privatization and commercialization programs in Nigeria were initiated to reduce the government's direct involvement in business enterprises. By transferring ownership and management of public enterprises to private entities, these programs aimed to enhance efficiency, stimulate economic growth, and alleviate the financial burden on the government. This strategic shift marked a significant move away from extensive government ownership toward a more market-driven economy.​

While these programs have had varied impacts, including debates on their influence on government revenue and concerns about corruption, their primary characteristic has been the reduction of government ownership in favor of private sector participation.

question: In the Igbo political system, the most senior member of the council of elders is the
solution:
In the Igbo political system, the most senior member of the council of elders is the Okpara. The Igbo political system was based on the village as a political unit.

A village is then seen as made up of a group of related families. Each family head held the Ofo title and all of them put together formed the council of elders. The council of elders governed the village. Among the Ofo title holders, one of them was regarded as the most senior to as the Okpara and he had the right to preside over the council of elders meetings.

question: The immediate cause of the 1966 military coup de'etat in Nigeria was
solution: The immediate cause of the 1966 military coup de'etat in Nigeria was political instability. It should be noted that the Action Group Crises of 1962 was one of the crisis that led to the military take-over of government in January 1966. The 1962 Action Group crisis continued to rear its ugly head, until Western Nigeria election crisis of 1965. The disagreement over the election result between UPGA and NNA in Western region resulted to a crisis which led to a breakdown of law and order. This situation attracted military intervention on 15th January, 1966.

question: The exclusive list is reserved for legislation by the
solution:
The exclusive list is reserved for legislation by the Federal Government. There are about forty three exclusive lists.

Concurrent powers are left for both federal and the state governments while residual lists are reserved for the states only.

question: The participation of Nigerians in the Second World War created in the nationalists the awareness that the

option.b: Africans were more qualified to rule
option.d: Europeans were less tactful in warfare

solution:
The Participation of Nigerians in the Second World War created in the nationalists the awareness that the Europeans were not superior.

Their participation in the war, made them to know more about the white people, and realized that they were in no way superior to Africans including Nigerians.

question: Prior to 1976, the local government system practiced in Northern Nigeria was
solution: Prior to 1976, the Local government system practised in Northern Nigerian Nigeria was Chief-in-Council.By 1952, the Northern region abolished the sole Native Authority and put in its place, Chief-in-Council and Chief-and-Council and. The democratization process in the Northern region was further aided by the Native Authority Law of 1954 which was amended by the Northern Nigeria Law of 1963. They provided for five Native Authorities including Chief-in-Council and the Federal Native Authorities.

question: Which of the following influenced nationalist activities in Nigeria?

option.d: National Congress of British West Africa

solution: In the history of Nigerian nationalism, regional organizations played a significant role in mobilizing and uniting educated elites and activists against colonial rule. The National Congress of British West Africa was one such organization that brought together nationalist leaders from across the British West African colonies, including Nigeria, to demand self-governance and greater political representation. This organization provided both a model and inspiration for the emerging nationalist activities in Nigeria.

question: The party that did not control any state in the Second Republic was
solution: NAP was the party that did not control any state in the Second Republic. Nigeria Advanced Party (NAP) was not recognized for the 1979 election. The reason was that it was unable to establish branches in 13 states of the federation as stipulated by FEDECO. In the 1983 general elections, which the party contested, it did not win a single seat in all the constituencies.

question: The AU's right to intervene in a member country in crisis portrays it as
solution: The AU's right to intervene in a member country in crisis portrays it as an international organization.

question: An advantage of flexible constitution is that it allows for
solution: An advantage of flexible constitution is that it allows for ease of amendment. Changes can be made easily in order to accommodate changing situations in the society. Its amendment does not require or involve much time and financial cost.

question: Power refers to

option.a: ability to impose one's will upon others
option.c: duties political parties demand of thir registered memebers
option.d: duties an individual imposes on himself

solution: Power refers to the ability to impose one's will upon others. It could also be defined as the ability of a person or a group of persons to enforce his or their decisions on others by the use of sanctions punishment or penalties against disobedience. According to Max Weber, "Power is the capability to control the actions of others"

question: Occupational pressure groups pursue their objectives mainly by

option.a: issuing ultimatums and embarking on strikes

solution: Occupational pressure groups pursue their objectives mainly by pursue their objectives mainly by issuing ultimatums and embarking on strikes. The pressure groups give, 7, 14 or 21 days ultimatum to the government at the end of this, any action which the group deems necessary will be taken against the government. If the demands of the pressure groups are not met at the end of the ultimatum given, they will have to embark on strikes and boycotts.

question: In a cabinet system, the legislature comprises of the

option.a: prime minister and members of the parliament
option.b: prime minister and members of the jury

solution:
In a cabinet system, the legislature comprises the prime minister and members of the parliament.

One of the characteristics of cabinet system of government is that the Prime Minister and all cabinet ministers are also members of the parliament or legislature.

question: The commitee that scrutinizes budget proposals in the legislature is the
solution: The Committee that scrutinizes budget proposals in the legislature is the Appropriation Committee.

question: Democracy is a system of government that is

option.a: constituted by the will of the majority of the citizens
option.b: imposed on the majority of the citizens
option.d: put in place to favour the party caucus

solution:
Democracy is a system of government that is constituted by the will of the majority of the citizens.

It could also be defined as government in accordance with the principles of popular sovereignty, political equality, popular consultation and majority rule. One of the characteristics of democracy is that all government actions must be open to criticisms and must be according to the decisions, wishes and aspirations of majority of the people.

question: Collective ownership of property is associated with
solution:
Collective ownership of property is associated with Communism .

It should be noted that Communism is a social system where the means of production, distribution and exchange are completely owned and managed by the State. The economy is operated centrally and individuals have little or no opportunity for private enterprises.

question: Which of the following is a duty of an electoral commission
solution: A core duty of an electoral commission is the registration of political parties to ensure they meet legal criteria (e.g., membership size, ideological compliance) for participating in elections. This process validates parties as legitimate contenders and maintains electoral integrity.

question: Fascism as an ideology facilitates
solution: Fascism as an ideology facilitates Totalitarianism. It is an Italian version of totalitarianism which grew up in Italy under Benito Mussolini

question: In order to dispense justice impartially, the judiciary primarily needs
solution: The judiciary needs independence primarily in order to dispense justice impartially. Judges must be free from the absolute control of either the executive or the legislature. That means that the judiciary should not be controlled any how.

question: A state with a hegemonic political party is one in which
solution: A State with a hegemonic political party is one in which there is only one dominant party. In this system, one party is firmly established in office. The party has hegemony, that is, it enjoys unchallenged authority. The law does not operate formally against any opposition, but clearly the state concerned is effectively single-party for longer period of many years. For example, South Africa was an example of a hegemonic party. There, the National party came into power in 1948, and it only left when Dr. Nelson Mandela was elected president in 1994.

question: Nigeria's commitment to the liberation of Africa could best be seen in her policy towards
solution: During the liberation struggles in Southern Africa, Nigeria was a leading voice in supporting anti-colonial and anti-apartheid movements. It provided both political and moral support to groups fighting apartheid in South Africa and to liberation movements in Angola. These policies were part of Nigeria’s broader commitment to Pan-Africanism and the liberation of oppressed peoples across the continent, making its stance toward South Africa and Angola a prominent example of this commitment.

question: The set of policies on the basis of which countries interact with one another is called
solution:
The set of policies on the basis of which countries interact with one another is called foreign policy.
It could also be defined as the sum total of decision formulated by the government of a country to manipulate the international environment to attain certain national interest or objective.

question: Nigeria's membership of the UN complements her foreign policy in the area of
solution: Nigeria's membership of the UN complements her foreign policy in the area of multilateral diplomacy.

question: How many states were created in Nigeria in 1967?
solution: In 1967, Nigeria was reorganized from four regions into 12 states by General Yakubu Gowon. This move was designed to reduce ethnic tensions, promote administrative efficiency, and weaken regional power bases that had contributed to the country's political instability.

question: A major function of Warrant Chiefs was to
solution: Under the colonial system of indirect rule in Nigeria, warrant chiefs were appointed by the British colonial authorities to serve as intermediaries between the colonial government and the indigenous communities. Their primary function was to manage local administration, including tax collection, maintenance of order, and implementation of colonial policies, thus ensuring that the colonial government’s interests were maintained at the local level.

question: A state that is ruled by an elected citizen is
solution: Republic is defined as a situation or process whereby the people democratically elect their leaders into public or political offices.

question: The main role of pressure groups is to

option.a: influence the legislature to make decision favourable to them
option.d: use all means, including bribery, to achieve their objectives

solution: The primary role of pressure groups is to advocate for specific policies or interests by influencing legislators and policymakers. They achieve this through lobbying, submitting proposals, organizing campaigns, and testifying at hearings. For example, environmental groups push for climate legislation, while trade unions advocate for labor rights.

question: The OAU was formed in
solution: The Organisation of African Unity (OAU) was formed on May 25, 1963, in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. It was established to promote unity and cooperation among African countries and to support the decolonization of African nations. It was later replaced by the African Union (AU) in 2001 to address more modern political and economic challenges.

question: The first African Secretary-General of the United Nations is
solution: This is history oriented. Boutros Boutros-Ghali is an Egyptian politician and diplomat who was the sixth, but the first African Secretary-General of the United Nations from January 1992 to December 1996 and was later succeeded by Kofi Annan.

question: Nigeria spearheaded the formation of ECOWAS during the regime of
solution: Nigeria played a pivotal role in the formation of the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS) during the regime of General Yakubu Gowon. In collaboration with Togolese President Gnassingbé Eyadéma, Gowon initiated efforts to promote economic integration in West Africa. Their joint diplomatic missions across the region culminated in the signing of the Treaty of Lagos on May 28, 1975, officially establishing ECOWAS. ​

question: France introduced the policy of assimilation in her colonies primarily to

option.a: teach them the art of leadership

solution: The French policy of assimilation was designed to transform the cultural identities of colonized peoples by encouraging them to adopt French language, customs, and laws. The underlying goal was to integrate colonial subjects into French society, effectively changing their traditional ways of life to mirror that of France. This approach was rooted in the belief that spreading French civilization would "civilize" the colonies, leading to the erosion of indigenous cultures and practices.

question: In the Oyo traditional political system, the Alafin of Oyo was elected or chosen by a group known as
solution: In the Oyo Empire’s traditional political system, the Oyo Mesi (a council of seven high-ranking chiefs) held the authority to elect or depose the Alafin. This council ensured checks and balances by selecting a new Alafin from the royal lineage and overseeing his governance.

question: A good example of a country with a largely unwritten constitution is
solution: The United Kingdom operates under an uncodified constitution, often referred to as an "unwritten" constitution. This means that, unlike countries with a single, consolidated constitutional document, the UK's constitutional framework is derived from multiple sources, including statutes, common law, conventions, and historical documents. While many elements are written, they are not compiled into a single document, distinguishing the UK from nations with codified constitutions

question: Before Nigeria became a republic, the highest body charged with the administration of justice was the
solution: Before Nigeria became a republic in 1963, the highest judicial authority was the Judicial Committee of the Privy Council in the United Kingdom. During this period, decisions from Nigeria's Federal Supreme Court could be appealed to the Privy Council, which served as the final court of appeal for British colonies and certain Commonwealth countries

question: The two leaders that played the most prominent roles in the formation of the ECOWAS were
solution: Both Leaders (Gen. Yakubu Gowon of Nigeria and Eyadema of Togo) used their military might to facilitate the organization.

question: A referendum is a device to ensure that

option.b: legislators vote to resolve contentious issues
option.c: decisions are effected with the consent of citizens
option.d: bye-elections are held to fill vacant positions

solution: A referendum is a direct voting process in which the electorate is invited to vote on a particular proposal and decide on issues such as constitutional amendments, changes in laws, or specific policies. This process ensures that decisions reflect the will of the citizens, as it requires their direct approval or disapproval. Unlike regular elections, which select representatives, referendums allow citizens to have a say on specific legislative or policy issues directly.

question: The Mid-Western Region was created in Nigeria in
solution: The Mid-Western Region was created in Nigeria on August 9, 1963. It was established as a result of the 1962 census and was the first new region to be created in Nigeria after independence. This change was part of a broader effort to accommodate Nigeria's diverse ethnic and cultural groups in the federal structure.

question: A form of oligarchy in which gifted people are at the helm of affairs is
solution:
Aristocracy refers to a form of government in which power is held by a small, privileged ruling class, typically consisting of individuals who are considered "gifted" or of noble birth. Aristocrats often possess superior social, economic or educational status and are believed to have the qualities necessary to govern effectively.

Theocracy is a form of government in which religious leaders control the government, claiming to be ruling on behalf of a deity. Plutocracy is a system of government where the wealthy class has significant power and influence over political decisions. Gerontocracy is a system where older individuals hold power, often based on age rather than merit or ability.

question: One of the principal objectives of the OPEC is to

option.a: determine oil prices in the international market
option.b: harmonise the oil policies of member countries

solution: One of the principal objectives of the Organisation of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) is to harmonise and coordinate the oil production policies of its member countries. This coordination helps to stabilise oil markets and ensure fair and stable prices for petroleum producers while securing a reliable supply for consumers. While OPEC does have some influence over oil prices in the international market (option A), its primary focus is on aligning the policies of its member states to achieve mutual benefits rather than directly setting prices

question: A constitution that requires a plebiscite or a referendum to be amended is
solution: A rigid constitution requires a special procedure (e.g., a plebiscite, referendum, or supermajority legislative vote) for amendment, distinguishing it from ordinary laws. This design protects fundamental principles from frequent or hasty changes.

question: The following are international organisations except
solution: International organizations are typically formal institutions where multiple countries cooperate to achieve common goals. Examples include the Organization of African Unity (now the African Union), the United Nations Organization (UNO), and the Commonwealth of Nations, which involve governments coming together to coordinate policies and address global or regional issues. In contrast, a pressure group is an interest group that seeks to influence government policy and decision-making but is not an international organization in the formal, intergovernmental sense.

question: The rights of a citizen can be withdrawn by the state if the person

option.c: is convicted of a serious crime

solution: In many constitutional democracies, while citizens generally enjoy a wide range of rights, these rights can be curtailed in certain circumstances. One common legal provision is that upon conviction of a serious crime, certain rights (such as the right to vote or hold public office) may be withdrawn or limited for the duration of the sentence or as stipulated by law.

question: n the Presidential System of Government, the president is elected to office by
solution: In a Presidential System of Government, the president is elected to office by the people through direct elections. This means that citizens have the opportunity to vote directly for their president, rather than the president being appointed or elected by the legislature, cabinet or military.

question: The act of removing an elected official by the electorate is referred to as
solution: A recall is a procedure that allows voters to remove an elected official from office before their term is completed. This process is initiated by the electorate, and if sufficient signatures or support is gathered, a recall election is held. This is distinct from impeachment, which is a legislative process used to remove officials from office for misconduct, and from plebiscite, which is a direct vote by the electorate on an important public question.

question: Which of the following world powers was the first to come to Nigeria’s aid during the civil war?
solution: During the Nigerian Civil War (1967–1970), Britain was the first world power to come to Nigeria's aid. They supported the Federal Government's military efforts. This support was largely driven by political, economic, and strategic interests, including the desire to maintain relations with Nigeria as a former colony and the geopolitical situation during the Cold War.

question: Citizenship refers to the

option.a: legal status of a member of a state
option.d: social status of a person in a state

solution: Citizenship refers to the legal recognition of a person as a member of a state. The word "legal" guarantees you the constitutional right to enjoy the benefits of the state.

question: The highest organ of the state during the Babangida Regime was the
solution:
The Supreme Military Council, SMC was the top highest organ of the Federal Government of Nigeria in the military regimes of Johnson Aguiyi-Ironsi, General Yakubu Gowon, Murtala Mohammed, General Obasanjo and Major General Muhammadu Buhari.
The body was instituted in 1966 by the administration of Aguiyi Ironsi deriving power from statute 8 of decree 1, it was later replaced as the highest body in a military regime by President Ibrahim Babangida in 1985 who created the Armed Forces Ruling Council.

question: The council constituted by Lord Lugard in 1914 has
solution: The council constituted by Lord Lugard in 1914, following the amalgamation of Northern and Southern Nigeria, had 36 members; 24 official and 12 unofficial members. This was part of his administrative structure aimed at unifying the two regions.

question: Nigeria’s withdrawal from the Edinburgh Commonwealth Games in July 1986 was in protest against British

option.b: failure to impose sanctions on South Africa
option.d: support for UNITA rebels in Angola

solution: Nigeria withdrew from the 1986 Edinburgh Commonwealth Games in protest against the British government's reluctance to impose sanctions on apartheid South Africa.
